channel.title: GeoPulse — Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefs on geopolitics, geography, and world history.

item.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
item.description: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

item.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Jeffrey Epstein and urges hearing for survivors
item.description: She called rumours that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump "mean-spirited attempts to defame my reputation".

item.title: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
item.description: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

item.title: German transgender far-right extremist arrested in Czech Republic
item.description: German media reports say Marla-Svenja Liebich used to be a member of a neo-Nazi group called Blood and Honour.

item.title: Man accused of coercing wife into sex with 120 men goes on trial in Sweden
item.description: The 61-year-old denies threatening physical violence and plying his wife with drugs to force her into having sex with strangers.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter truce
item.description: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday.

item.title: Popular Tanzanian musician Matonya charged with rape in Kenya
item.description: The 43-year-old denies the charge and has been released on bail and had to surrender his passport.

item.title: White House staff told not to place bets on prediction markets
item.description: The gambling platforms have grown in popularity, with some users making wagers on conflicts.

item.title: Dutch police urge victims of 'international sextortion campaign' to come forward
item.description: Dutch police have warned that dozens of women and girls were coerced into degrading sexual acts on camera in several countries.

item.title: US has let in 4,499 refugees since October - all but three were South African
item.description: Trump has given priority to Afrikaners, a white minority group he says is being persecuted. South Africa has objected to his characterisation.

item.title: Netanyahu removes Spain from Gaza coordination centre over ‘hostility’
item.description: Israel's Netanyahu accused Spain of waging a 'diplomatic war' against Israel.

item.title: JD Vance expects ‘positive’ US-Iran war talks as he departs for Pakistan
item.description: Vice president says Trump gave 'pretty clear guidelines' on talks, which will be productive if Iran does not 'play' US.

item.title: LIVE: Real Madrid vs Girona – La Liga
item.description: Follow our live build-up, with full team news coverage, before our text commentary as Real look to close on Barcelona.

item.title: US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad face massive hurdles
item.description: Ali Vaez, says upcoming talks between the US and Iran may be marred by a deficit of trust.

item.title: In Peru’s presidential race, a pro-Trump frontrunner sinks in final stretch
item.description: Donald Trump has sought greater influence over Latin America, but in Peru's election, will a pro-Trump stance pay off?

item.title: Potholes and progress: Mamdani reflects on 100 days as New York’s mayor
item.description: Mamdani's first 100 days marked by childcare reforms, pothole fixes and polarising debates over affordability policies.

item.title: Inflation rises in US amid Iran war, Hormuz blockade
item.description: Government report shows gasoline going up by 21.2 percent in March as petrol remains above $4 per gallon despite truce.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukrainian interceptors downed Iran drones in the Middle East
item.description: Ukraine's Zelenskyy previously announced that 228 Ukrainian experts had been deployed to the region.

item.title: Pakistan sets modest goal for US-Iran summit: A deal to keep talks going
item.description: US-Iran talks open in Islamabad with mediators focused on keeping dialogue alive amid deep divisions.

item.title: Artemis II splashdown: When it lands, risks and how to watch live
item.description: The Orion spacecraft will make its return to Earth at 00:07 GMT, splashing down off the San Diego coast.
